vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-805: Epic A — Labels as display, internal state as source of truth

plan.title: Epic A: Labels as display, internal state as source of truth
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: GitHub labels are used as the state-machine source of truth via fire-and-forget writes with no idempotency, no audit, and five boot-time repair sweeps that flood the API on every restart. Silent rate-limit drops cause label drift (PAN-676, PAN-698).
narratives.Proposal: Introduce a reconciler service that owns all label writes. Internal SQLite state becomes authoritative, labels become a display mirror. Bidirectional sync: push local intent, pull remote changes. Every API attempt is audited. Delete the boot repair sweeps; migrate all direct-write call sites through the reconciler queue; add CI enforcement to prevent regression.

items.title: Add issue_state and label_sync_audit tables to schema.ts

narrative.Action: Bump SCHEMA_VERSION from 27 to 28 in src/lib/database/schema.ts. Add the two new tables with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS plus an index on label_sync_audit(issue_id, attempted_at). Create a new db accessor module src/lib/database/issue-state-db.ts exporting typed getters/setters for issue_state and an audit writer for label_sync_audit.

subItems.title: issue_state table exists with columns issue_id (PK), canonical_state, last_synced_at, pending_mutation, updated_at

label_sync_audit table exists with id (PK AUTOINCREMENT), issue_id, attempted_at, target_label, action (CHECK add|remove), outcome (CHECK success|failure|rate_limited|skipped), reason, retry_count DEFAULT 0, http_status
SCHEMA_VERSION bumped to 28 and schema init is idempotent (running initSchema twice does not error)
issue-state-db.ts exports getIssueState(id), upsertIssueState(row), writeAuditRow(entry) with strict TypeScript types

items.title: Create reconciler module scaffold with loop, mutex, and config

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/ with index.ts (public API), loop.ts (fixed-interval tick driver + single-instance in-process mutex), config.ts (reads PANOPTICON_RECONCILER_INTERVAL_MS env var, default 30000). loop.ts exports startReconciler() and stopReconciler(); each tick is wrapped in try/catch so one failure does not kill the loop. No push/pull/sweep logic in this bead — just the runner and an empty tick fn.

subItems.title: startReconciler() begins ticking at the configured interval; stopReconciler() clears the timer cleanly
Calling startReconciler() twice without stopping is a no-op (mutex prevents double-start) and logs a WARN
PANOPTICON_RECONCILER_INTERVAL_MS env var overrides the default 30000; invalid values fall back to default with a WARN log
A thrown error inside the tick fn is caught and logged; the next tick still runs

items.title: Reconciler GitHub client with .ok check and 429/5xx retry

narrative.Action: Add src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/github-client.ts — a thin wrapped fetch for label add/remove, issue state fetch, and list-issues. Every call checks response.ok; on 429 or 5xx retries with exponential backoff (initial 1s, cap 60s, max 5 attempts); honors Retry-After header when present. Emits structured error logs with issueId, operation, status, body snippet. Never swallows errors — throws after max retries.

subItems.title: Every fetch call checks response.ok; non-ok responses throw or trigger retry
On 429 or 5xx: exponential backoff 1s → 2s → 4s → ... capped at 60s, max 5 attempts
Retry-After header (seconds or HTTP-date) overrides the backoff schedule when present
Errors include issueId, operation, status, and a body snippet in the log; after max retries, the error is thrown (not swallowed)

items.title: Audit writer records every attempt to label_sync_audit

narrative.Action: Add src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/audit.ts. Exposes writeAudit({issueId, targetLabel, action, outcome, reason, retryCount, httpStatus}). Called from every terminal point in the reconciler (success, failure after max retries, rate-limited final, skipped). Integrates with issue-state-db.writeAuditRow from the schema bead.

subItems.title: Every push attempt (success, failure, rate_limited, skipped) writes exactly one audit row per intent
retry_count reflects the number of retries attempted before the terminal outcome
http_status is populated for success/failure/rate_limited rows; NULL for skipped (no API call made)

items.title: Pure function mapping canonical_state → desired label set

narrative.Action: Add src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/desired-labels.ts exporting desiredLabelsFor(state: CanonicalState): string[]. Pure, unit-tested. States: todo, in_progress, in_review, merged, closed_wontfix. This is the single source of truth for which labels a state implies.

subItems.title: desiredLabelsFor returns the exact current Panopticon label convention for each canonical state
Unit tests cover all 5 canonical states

items.title: Reconciler push step: diff issue_state vs remote, write deltas

narrative.Action: Add src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/push.ts. Each tick: for each row in issue_state where pending_mutation IS NOT NULL (or canonical_state changed since last_synced_at), compute desired labels, diff against last-synced, and write only the add/remove deltas via the github-client. On success, clear pending_mutation and update last_synced_at. Every attempt writes an audit row via audit.ts. No-op diffs write audit with outcome=skipped reason=no_diff.

subItems.title: Only deltas are written — if desired == last-synced, no API call is made
On successful write, pending_mutation is cleared and last_synced_at is advanced
On final failure after max retries, pending_mutation is retained so the next tick retries
Every terminal outcome writes exactly one audit row

items.title: Reconciler pull step: list-issues, detect remote-ahead, update local

narrative.Action: Add src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/pull.ts. Each tick: one paginated call to GET /repos/:o/:r/issues with labels filter scoped to Panopticon-known labels and state!=closed (or state=all excluding terminal canonical states locally). For each issue: if remote-implied canonical state differs from local AND no pending_mutation, update issue_state.canonical_state + last_synced_at and write audit row with outcome=skipped reason=remote_ahead_pulled. If pending_mutation exists and states conflict, log WARN with both states and let the pending write proceed.

subItems.title: Pull uses list-issues with pagination per tick (scope narrowed from spec: active/non-terminal issues only, per planning decision recorded in STATE.md), NOT one fetch per issue
Remote-ahead detection updates local canonical_state and last_synced_at; writes audit row with reason=remote_ahead_pulled
If a pending_mutation exists and remote state conflicts, WARN log is emitted with both states and the pending local write is preserved (not overwritten)
Issues in terminal canonical states locally are excluded from pull-sync to bound API usage

items.title: External merge sweep: detect closed-on-GitHub issues missing the merged label

narrative.Action: Add src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/external-merge-sweep.ts. Each tick: find issues where remote state=closed (from pull data) but local canonical_state != merged and no merged label present. Update local canonical_state to merged and set pending_mutation so the push step labels them. Audit row carries reason=external_merge_detected.

subItems.title: Issues closed on GitHub without a merged label are detected each tick
Detection flips local canonical_state to merged and enqueues the merged label via pending_mutation
Audit row for the resulting label write carries reason=external_merge_detected

items.title: Boot-time backfill of issue_state from local + GitHub

narrative.Action: On dashboard boot, before starting the reconciler loop: enumerate issues known locally (workspaces + agent state), and for any not present in issue_state, fetch their current label state from GitHub once (batched where possible) and insert a row. This runs once per boot, is idempotent, and populates canonical_state from the labels observed.

subItems.title: On boot, any locally-known issue missing from issue_state gets a row whose canonical_state matches current GitHub labels
Backfill is idempotent — running it twice produces no duplicate rows and no extra API calls
Backfill runs before the first reconciler tick to avoid spurious pull-sync updates racing the backfill

items.title: Wire reconciler startup into dashboard boot

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/main.ts, call the backfill and then startReconciler() during boot. Also register stopReconciler() on shutdown hooks. Do NOT include any repair* wiring (those are removed in separate beads).

subItems.title: Dashboard boot runs backfill then starts the reconciler loop
Clean shutdown stops the reconciler loop (no dangling timers)

items.title: Delete all 5 repair* functions and their tests

narrative.Action: Remove repairMergedLabels, repairAlreadyMergedPRs, repairIncompletePostMergeLifecycle, repairClosedWontfixIssues, repairClosedPRs from src/lib/lifecycle/label-cleanup.ts. Remove the matching test files. Delete the file if no exports remain. npm run build and npm run typecheck must pass.

subItems.title: All 5 repair* functions deleted from label-cleanup.ts (not commented out)
Associated test files deleted; npm test passes without them
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass without any of the repair* symbols referenced anywhere

grep confirms no remaining references to repairMergedLabels, repairAlreadyMergedPRs, repairIncompletePostMergeLifecycle, repairClosedWontfixIssues, or repairClosedPRs anywhere in src/ or tests/ (imports, callers, comments, or doc references)

items.title: Remove repair* wiring from dashboard boot path

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/main.ts (around line 154), delete the commented-out repair* calls and any surrounding dead imports. Do this cleanly — no // removed comments left behind.

subItems.title: src/dashboard/server/main.ts no longer references any repair* symbol (commented or otherwise)
No orphan imports left behind; npm run lint passes

items.title: Migrate transitionIssueToInProgress to reconciler with idempotency + lazy insert

narrative.Action: In src/lib/agents.ts:~1058, replace the direct label write in transitionIssueToInProgress. New flow: (1) read issue_state.canonical_state; (2) if already in_progress, log INFO issueId=X already in-progress, skipping and return with no API call; (3) if row is missing, lazy-insert it with canonical_state=in_progress; (4) otherwise update canonical_state to in_progress and set pending_mutation so the reconciler push step writes the label.

subItems.title: transitionIssueToInProgress reads issue_state.canonical_state before acting
When canonical_state is already in_progress, no API call is made and an INFO log is emitted with the expected format
If the issue has no issue_state row, one is lazy-inserted (covers issues missed by boot backfill)
Actual label writes flow through the reconciler queue, not a direct fetch from this call site

items.title: Migrate updateGitHubToInReview to reconciler queue

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/done.ts (note: spec cites src/dashboard/server/done.ts but the current code lives in cli/commands/done.ts — work agent should verify path), route the in-review transition through the reconciler. Any remaining direct fetch gets an explicit .ok check + structured error log (issueId, operation, status, body snippet) and throws instead of swallowing the error.

subItems.title: updateGitHubToInReview sets canonical_state=in_review and lets the reconciler perform the label write
Any surviving direct fetch call has an explicit .ok check
Errors are thrown (not swallowed) and logs include issueId, operation, status, and a body snippet

items.title: Migrate bulk-close endpoint (PAN-569) to reconciler queue

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts, update the bulk-close endpoint to set canonical_state=closed_wontfix (or whichever state the endpoint maps to) on each targeted issue and enqueue via the reconciler. Remove direct label write fetch calls from this endpoint.

subItems.title: Bulk-close endpoint no longer performs direct GitHub label fetch calls
Each targeted issue has its canonical_state updated and pending_mutation set so the reconciler writes the label
Endpoint response is unchanged from the client's perspective (UI contract preserved)

items.title: Remove Closes #NNN from PR body template/generator

narrative.Action: Grep the codebase for every emitter of 'Closes #', 'Fixes #', or 'Resolves #' in PR body generation paths. Remove the directive. Keep the human-readable issue link if desired, but no GitHub auto-close keywords.

subItems.title: No PR body emitter in the codebase produces Closes #, Fixes #, or Resolves # directives
Existing tests that assert on PR body content are updated accordingly

items.title: postMergeLifecycle explicitly closes issue via API and enqueues merged label

narrative.Action: In src/lib/lifecycle/merge-agent.ts (postMergeLifecycle) or equivalent post-merge path: after a successful merge, call PATCH /repos/:o/:r/issues/:n with state=closed (via the github-client for .ok + retry semantics), AND set canonical_state=merged + pending_mutation so the reconciler applies the merged label.

subItems.title: After a Panopticon-driven merge, the issue is closed via explicit GitHub API call (not relying on Closes # keyword)
The close call goes through the github-client so .ok + retry + Retry-After semantics apply
canonical_state is set to merged and the merged label is enqueued via the reconciler

items.title: Grep-based CI check prevents label-write regressions outside reconciler

narrative.Action: Add scripts/check-label-writes.sh (or .ts) that greps for 'gh issue edit' and direct fetch calls to /labels endpoints anywhere in src/ outside src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/**. Wire it into the CI pipeline (package.json script + CI workflow file). Exits non-zero on any hit.

subItems.title: A CI-invoked script fails the build if gh issue edit or direct label-write fetch appears outside src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/**
Script is wired into CI (package.json + workflow) and runs on every PR
Script passes cleanly on the migrated codebase

items.title: Test: respawn flood — 1000x transitionIssueToInProgress yields 0 API calls

narrative.Action: Vitest integration test under src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/__tests__/. Seed issue_state with canonical_state=in_progress for one issue. Mock GitHub. Call transitionIssueToInProgress 1000 times sequentially. Assert: the GitHub mock receives exactly 0 label-write calls.

subItems.title: Test calls transitionIssueToInProgress 1000 times with canonical_state already in_progress
Assertion: GitHub mock receives 0 label-write calls
Test passes in CI

items.title: Test: rate-limit recovery — 429 Retry-After handling and audit trail

narrative.Action: Vitest test. Mock GitHub to return 429 Retry-After: 2 for the first 3 calls, then 200. Use fake timers to advance through the backoff. Enqueue a label write and drive the reconciler. Assert: final outcome is success; label_sync_audit shows retry_count >= 3 and a final row with outcome=success.

subItems.title: Mock returns 429 Retry-After: 2 for first 3 attempts then 200
Reconciler eventually succeeds on the 4th attempt
label_sync_audit contains a row with retry_count >= 3 and outcome=success

items.title: Test: external merge sweep labels issues merged via GitHub web UI

narrative.Action: Vitest test. Mock an issue+PR where the PR is closed+merged without Panopticon involvement (issue_state.canonical_state != merged locally). Drive a reconciler tick. Assert: canonical_state updates to merged, merged label is applied (push step), audit row written with reason=external_merge_detected.

subItems.title: Scenario: GitHub reports PR merged, issue closed, no merged label locally
After one tick: canonical_state=merged, merged label applied
label_sync_audit row has reason=external_merge_detected

items.title: Test: PR body generator emits no Closes #NNN / Fixes # / Resolves # directives

narrative.Action: Vitest test. Invoke the Panopticon PR-body code path for a representative issue. Assert: the generated body contains no occurrences of Closes #, Fixes #, or Resolves # (case-insensitive).

subItems.title: Test calls the PR-body generator for at least one representative scenario
Assertion: output contains no Closes #, Fixes #, or Resolves # (case-insensitive)

items.title: Test: CI enforcement fails when a stray gh issue edit is added

narrative.Action: Shell or Vitest test that writes a fixture file under a temp path containing a stray 'gh issue edit' call (or a direct /labels fetch) and invokes the CI enforcement script with that file in scope. Assert: non-zero exit. Then remove the fixture and assert: zero exit.

subItems.title: Fixture with a stray gh issue edit call outside the reconciler path causes the enforcement script to fail (non-zero exit)
Fixture with a stray direct /labels fetch call outside the reconciler path causes the enforcement script to fail
Without the fixture, the script passes cleanly on the migrated codebase

items.title: Test: multi-developer pull-sync detects remote-ahead state

narrative.Action: Vitest test. Seed local issue_state with canonical_state=in_progress for an issue. Mock GitHub list-issues to return that issue with labels implying in_review (as if another developer's Panopticon transitioned it). Drive one reconciler tick. Assert: local canonical_state=in_review; last_synced_at advances; label_sync_audit has outcome=skipped reason=remote_ahead_pulled.

subItems.title: Seeded local state is in_progress; mocked remote labels imply in_review
After one tick, local canonical_state=in_review and last_synced_at advances
label_sync_audit row has outcome=skipped and reason=remote_ahead_pulled
